title: Strait and Narrow

mood.label: Tense and volatile
mood.summary: A world at war on multiple fronts — Iran closes the Strait of Hormuz, Hezbollah rejects a ceasefire as an Israeli soldier dies on its first day, Iranian missiles strike Kuwait, and the AI governance clock starts ticking while the real clocks count casualties.

sampleSources: Strudel default library — sampled piano, vibraphone (VCSL), RolandTR808

lines: The strait goes quiet, the tankers hold their breath,
a price on every barrel, a cost no ledger keeps.
One soldier down in Lebanon on ceasefire's opening day —
a truce is just a word when the ground beneath it creeps.
A missile finds the runway at the edge of Kuwait,
Bahrain intercepts the rest before the sirens fade.
In Washington they sign a page on thinking machines,
thirty days to screen the mind that humans never made.
Strait and narrow, the passage closing down,
every handshake hides a fist beneath the table.
We build the ceasefire out of paper and goodwill —
tense and volatile, but talking while we're able.
